meta.setting: Boardinghouse kitchens, mine breakfasts, long tables, stove rooms, and back doors around French Gulch
meta.rdr_clone_target: RDR-clone western with food, rumor, shelter, sickness, debt, and hard choices

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Mining communities, immigrant settlement, work routines, logging, ranching, and lived regional context.

research_grounding.title: California State Parks Shasta State Historic Park
research_grounding.use: Northern California mining-town commerce, lodging, old interiors, and social texture.

generation_prompt.task: Generate the Boardinghouse Cooks cast page for the 1899 Jefferson State western packet. This JSON contains shape and guidance only.
page_shape: square image reference immediately after the title
opening blockquote with invented kitchen note, breakfast warning, or stove saying generated later
H2 archetype subtitle in the register of Stove Women and Breakfast Men
one or two compact lore paragraphs introducing cooks who feed miners, hear everything, control comfort, and decide who gets an extra plate
H3 Role with a short table-facing function phrase
H3 Traits with two to four terse signs or habits
10 H4 headings, each followed later by a brief playable move, leverage, cost, edge, or complication
H3 Camp Say closing blockquote with plain meaning generated later
generation_guidance: Generate boardinghouse cooks as logistical powers with food, rumor, medicine, debt, shelter, kitchen discipline, and quiet alliances.
Tie future moves to meals, ration scarcity, overheard talk, sickness, hiding someone in a pantry, breakfast order, kitchen knives, spoiled meat, and who gets served first.
Keep them earthy, funny, tired, sharp, and hard to fool.
Make the kitchen warmer than the town and twice as dangerous to liars.
Do not include final move names or final move text in this JSON.
generation_prompt.hand_cut_note: A person who controls breakfast controls the first truth of the day.
generation_prompt.table_spark: A boardinghouse scene should ask who is hungry, who is listening, who got a second plate, and whose name made the kitchen go quiet.
must_preserve: Use 1899 western kitchen, lodging, and work-camp language only.
Keep food practical, social, and powerful.
The generated page must include image path cast/images/boardinghouse-cooks.png.
No finished page prose, final moves, or final sayings belong in this prompt file.
avoid: do not mention any prior packet, inherited taxonomy, or non-western source language
do not include medieval, enchanted, sacred, or fake-tribal framing
do not make cooks quaint, comic-only, or sentimental
do not use modern kitchen appliances, modern slang, vehicles, or clean theme-park staging
self_check: The future markdown follows the exact heading sequence.
The future page is about food, rumor, shelter, sickness, social leverage, and table action.
The prompt contains no finished page prose.
All language belongs to grounded 1899 Jefferson State western play.
